description: Rank ordering of assets by review priority.
derivation_or_logic: Calculated after scoring all assets; 1 is the highest screener_attention_priority_score.
recommended_use: Use to sort the workbook and decide which assets to review first.
cautions: Priority is review urgency, not bullishness. Pair with direction label.

description: Asset ticker / term identifier.
derivation_or_logic: Primary join key across screener, matrix, archetype, chart history and alert files.
recommended_use: Use as context, join key, filter, or supporting diagnostic.

description: Asset universe classification, such as crypto or equity-like.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from sector map, asset calendar or term classification.
recommended_use: Use as context, join key, filter, or supporting diagnostic.

description: Primary sector or asset grouping.
derivation_or_logic: Pulled from available sector mapping where present.
recommended_use: Use as context, join key, filter, or supporting diagnostic.

neutral_anchor: No fixed neutral; higher means review sooner
description: Top-level triage score indicating how urgently an asset deserves review.
derivation_or_logic: Combines absolute signal consensus, recency, latest event quality, confirmed/event activity and attention/participation. It is not bullish or bearish by itself.
recommended_use: Use to sort the workbook and decide which assets to review first.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Human-readable triage bucket for the asset.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from recency, event tier, quality, watch/confirmed activity and priority score.
recommended_use: Use as context, join key, filter, or supporting diagnostic.
example_values: Fresh Confirmed Event; High-Quality Watch; Monitor

description: Main setup archetype assigned to the asset.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from rule-based combinations of events, consensus, attention, MACD/RSI/ribbon and dispersion.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.
example_values: Fresh Bullish Reversal; Sentiment Repair Ahead of Price; Narrative Deterioration Despite Price Strength

description: Secondary setup archetype if another meaningful pattern also matches.
derivation_or_logic: Used to capture nuance without overriding the primary archetype.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.
example_values: Watch Cluster Building; Quiet Neutral; Overextended / Exhaustion Risk

description: Categorical label for archetype direction.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from source labels or rule-based classification.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.

description: Categorical label for archetype confidence.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from source labels or rule-based classification.
recommended_use: Use to separate clean setups from noisy or conflicted ones.

description: Plain-English explanation of the matched archetype.
derivation_or_logic: Used downstream for verbal narrative generation.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.
example_values: SOL has a fresh confirmed bullish overlap event with supportive Bollinger pressure.; AVAX has a fresh confirmed bullish overlap event with supportive Bollinger pressure.; XRP has a fresh confirmed bullish overlap event with supportive Bollinger pressure.

description: Risk or caveat associated with the archetype.
derivation_or_logic: Highlights missing confirmation, stale signals, dispersion or exhaustion risk.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.
example_values: Mean-reversion/reversal setups still need price follow-through after the confirmed event.; Early repair signals are useful but less reliable until price MACD confirms.; This can be an early warning rather than an immediate breakdown signal.

description: Plain-English reason string explaining why the asset ranked where it did.
derivation_or_logic: Generated from active reason flags, dominant archetypes, signal state, attention and dispersion.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.
example_values: SOL ranked high because: fresh confirmed bullish overlap event; clustered recent overlap/watch activity; MACD family improving; low dispersion; consensus direction bullish.; AVAX ranked high because: fresh confirmed bullish overlap event; clustered recent overlap/watch activity; MACD family improving; low dispersion; consensus direction bullish.; XRP ranked high because: fresh confirmed bullish overlap event; clustered recent overlap/watch activity; MACD family improving; low dispersion; consensus direction bullish.

description: 0-100 directional consensus score across the major families.
derivation_or_logic: Uses neutral-centered family scores. 0 is bearish, 50 is neutral/mixed, 100 is bullish.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Categorical label for signal consensus direction label.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from source labels or rule-based classification.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.

description: Signed version of signal consensus.
derivation_or_logic: Computed from family scores as deviation from neutral. Positive is bullish, negative is bearish, 0 is neutral.
recommended_use: Use as context, join key, filter, or supporting diagnostic.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: How much the signal families disagree.
derivation_or_logic: Standard deviation-like dispersion across family direction scores. Low dispersion means cleaner agreement; high dispersion means a conflicted setup.
recommended_use: Use to separate clean setups from noisy or conflicted ones.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Confidence in the consensus direction.

description: Neutral-centered score for active overlap/Bollinger pressure.
derivation_or_logic: 50 means price is mostly inside the overlap range; above 50 bullish pressure; below 50 bearish pressure. Fresh confirmed events and watch clusters push it away from 50; time decay fades it back toward 50.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Confidence that the overlap/Bollinger condition is meaningful.
derivation_or_logic: Built from alert tier, quality, confirmed rate, volume confirmation, recency and event clustering.
recommended_use: Use to separate clean setups from noisy or conflicted ones.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: How fresh the latest Bollinger/overlap event is.
derivation_or_logic: Decay multiplier used to fade event-driven Bollinger pressure back toward neutral over time.
recommended_use: Use to evaluate overlap/Bollinger event pressure and confirmation quality.

description: Incremental score from repeated watch candidates.
derivation_or_logic: Counts clustered recent watch candidates so repeated developing pressure can matter without being treated like confirmed alerts.
recommended_use: Use to evaluate overlap/Bollinger event pressure and confirmation quality.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Bollinger direction score adjusted by participation/attention.
derivation_or_logic: Keeps the pure Bollinger score separate, then buffs or nerfs it when attention/engagement confirms or contradicts the directional setup.
recommended_use: Use to evaluate overlap/Bollinger event pressure and confirmation quality.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Classical price MACD directional score.
derivation_or_logic: Uses raw/public-comparable MACD, MACD signal, histogram, histogram slope, signal slope and cross information. 50 is neutral, above 50 bullish, below 50 bearish.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Sentiment MACD directional score.
derivation_or_logic: Measures whether sentiment momentum itself is improving or deteriorating. A high value means sentiment momentum is bullish, not merely aligned with price.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Score for relative sentiment-versus-price MACD momentum.
derivation_or_logic: Measures whether sentiment MACD signal is stronger or weaker than price MACD signal after normalization for relationship scoring.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Raw spread between scaled sentiment MACD signal and price MACD signal.
derivation_or_logic: Positive means sentiment MACD signal is above price MACD signal on the scaled comparison basis; negative means below.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.

description: Standardized sentiment-price MACD spread.
derivation_or_logic: Used only for SETA relationship scoring, not as a replacement for public-comparable price MACD values.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Z-scores are SETA relationship/normalization aids, not public-site TA values.

description: Change in the sentiment-price MACD spread.
derivation_or_logic: Positive means sentiment momentum is improving relative to price; negative means it is deteriorating relative to price.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.

description: Latest sentiment-over-price MACD signal crossover direction.
derivation_or_logic: Bullish when scaled sentiment MACD signal crosses above price MACD signal; bearish when it crosses below.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.

description: Days since the last sentiment-price MACD crossover.
derivation_or_logic: Used to decay crossover impact toward neutral as the event ages.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.

description: Neutral-centered event score for sentiment MACD signal crossing price MACD signal.
derivation_or_logic: 10% component of macd_family_direction_score. Fresh bullish crosses score above 50, bearish crosses below 50, and old crosses fade toward 50.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Score for whether price and sentiment MACD are rising/falling together.
derivation_or_logic: Captures both-rising, both-falling, sentiment repair ahead of price, and narrative weakening despite price strength.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Categorical label for the sentiment-price MACD slope relationship.
derivation_or_logic: Examples include Both Rising, Both Falling, Sentiment Repair / Price Lagging, and Price Rising / Sentiment Deteriorating.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.
example_values: Both Rising; Sentiment Repair / Price Lagging; Price Rising / Sentiment Deteriorating

description: Overall sentiment-enhanced MACD family direction score.
derivation_or_logic: Weighted blend: 30% price MACD direction, 25% sentiment MACD direction, 20% joint slope, 15% sentiment-price spread, 10% crossover score.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Interpretive label for the MACD family setup.

description: Categorical label for archetype direction.

description: Categorical label for archetype confidence.

description: Categorical label for archetype priority boost.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from source labels or rule-based classification.

description: Plain-English explanation of the matched archetype.

description: Risk or caveat associated with the archetype.
derivation_or_logic: Highlights missing confirmation, stale signals, dispersion or exhaustion risk.
recommended_use: Use for verbal summaries, dashboard cards and explanation layers.
example_values: Mean-reversion/reversal setups still need price follow-through after the confirmed event.; Early repair signals are useful but less reliable until price MACD confirms.; This can be an early warning rather than an immediate breakdown signal.

description: Semicolon-delimited list of rules that matched.
derivation_or_logic: Shows why the archetype was assigned.
recommended_use: Use as context, join key, filter, or supporting diagnostic.

description: All archetypes matched by the asset.

neutral_anchor: No fixed neutral; higher means review sooner
description: Top-level triage score indicating how urgently an asset deserves review.
derivation_or_logic: Combines absolute signal consensus, recency, latest event quality, confirmed/event activity and attention/participation. It is not bullish or bearish by itself.
recommended_use: Use to sort the workbook and decide which assets to review first.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: 0-100 directional consensus score across the major families.
derivation_or_logic: Uses neutral-centered family scores. 0 is bearish, 50 is neutral/mixed, 100 is bullish.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Categorical label for signal consensus direction label.
derivation_or_logic: Derived from source labels or rule-based classification.
recommended_use: Use to understand bullish versus bearish directional bias.

description: How much the signal families disagree.
derivation_or_logic: Standard deviation-like dispersion across family direction scores. Low dispersion means cleaner agreement; high dispersion means a conflicted setup.
recommended_use: Use to separate clean setups from noisy or conflicted ones.
cautions: Scores are model-derived interpretation aids; inspect raw values and reasons for context.

description: Interpretive label for the MACD family setup.
derivation_or_logic: Summarizes the MACD relationship, such as Bullish Confirmation, Bearish Confirmation, Positive Divergence / Sentiment Repair, or Negative Divergence / Narrative Weakening.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.
example_values: Bullish Confirmation; Positive Divergence / Sentiment Repair; Negative Divergence / Narrative Weakening

description: Categorical label for the sentiment-price MACD slope relationship.
derivation_or_logic: Examples include Both Rising, Both Falling, Sentiment Repair / Price Lagging, and Price Rising / Sentiment Deteriorating.
recommended_use: Use for momentum diagnostics and sentiment-enhanced MACD interpretation.
cautions: Raw MACD fields remain conventional; sentiment-price relationship fields are SETA-specific.
example_values: Both Rising; Sentiment Repair / Price Lagging; Price Rising / Sentiment Deteriorating

description: Interpretive label for the RSI family setup.
derivation_or_logic: Examples include RSI Constructive, RSI Weak, RSI Mixed / Neutral and exhaustion/repair style states.
recommended_use: Use for state, timing, divergence and exhaustion/repair diagnostics.

example_values: RSI Mixed / Neutral; RSI Constructive; Unknown
